justification: All pass criteria met: 4 bounded contexts identified (Route Planning, Fleet & Driver Management, Order Fulfillment, Customer Notification). Cross-context dependencies explicitly identified — e.g., route recalculation triggered by delivery lifecycle state changes, vehicle class constraint spanning Fleet & Route contexts, notification triggered by route position changes. Vehicle class rule correctly marked invariant (FD-1), stop time correctly marked non-invariant (RP-5), notification window implicitly treated as configurable. Route recalculation triggers correctly enumerated (shift start, new delivery, failed delivery, manual deviation). 5 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria plus extensive edge cases.

observations.text: Excellent bounded context decomposition with clear entity ownership and responsibility boundaries
Rich ubiquitous language section with precise definitions that disambiguate potentially confusing terms
Vehicle class constraint correctly identified as HARD SAFETY/LEGAL invariant with cross-context implications (FD-1, FD-2)
Stop time (RP-5) and notification window correctly classified as soft/configurable, not invariants
Cross-context dependencies surfaced as integration points — recalculation triggers span Order Fulfillment and Route Planning, notification depends on route position changes
Critical gaps section identifies genuine spec ambiguities (multi-driver assignment, deviation threshold, recalculation throttling) that would cause real implementation bugs
Edge cases are exceptionally thorough — race conditions, empty zones, unassignable deliveries, mid-shift config changes
Duplicate notification prevention rule (CN-2) shows deep domain understanding of event-driven notification pitfalls

Cross-context dependencies could be more formally structured as explicit integration contracts rather than being embedded within rule descriptions — MJ would benefit from a dedicated 'Integration Points' section

# Domain Analysis: Smart Route Optimization

## Domain Analysis

### Business Context

This feature encodes the **dynamic route planning and assignment process** for a last-mile delivery operation. It sits at the intersection of workforce management (driver shifts), order fulfillment (pending deliveries), and real-time logistics optimization (route calculation and recalculation).
